interpretation_summary: The preregistered TVA analysis was executed on an independent 2020–2021 dataset mirroring the original Experiment 1 (Cue vs NoCue). After a minor filename regex fix, the script estimated v and t0 per subject-condition and ran paired t-tests. Results did not replicate: both v and t0 effects were non-significant and in the opposite direction to the original. Multiple subject-level anomalies (negative t0, extreme v, ceiling accuracy) documented in the notes likely contributed. Overall, methods largely aligned, but estimation (SSE vs MLE) and data quality differences reduce fidelity.

fidelity_assessment.method_alignment: High-level alignment: TVA parameters (v, t0, pg) estimated per subject and condition; paired t-tests for Cue vs NoCue as preregistered. Parsing of E-Prime Excel logs and combining across days followed the plan. Deviations include use of SSE minimization rather than MLE and unconstrained parameter bounds, plus reliance on an independent dataset with documented data-quality variability.

deviations.issue_description: Used SSE-based fitting (scipy.optimize.minimize) instead of maximum-likelihood estimation used in the original; parameters unconstrained (e.g., negative t0, extreme v/pg).
deviations.impact: Moderate: Can yield unstable fits and inflate variance, potentially attenuating or inverting condition differences.

deviations.issue_description: Independent dataset with heterogeneous quality (e.g., headphone noncompliance, single-session cases, ceiling accuracy; many negative t0 values).
deviations.impact: High: Likely increased noise and biased parameter estimates, reducing power and possibly reversing effects.

deviations.issue_description: Minor code/IO changes (regex and directory search) to discover files.
deviations.impact: Low: Necessary for execution; unlikely to bias results.

results_comparison.hypothesis_tested: Phasic auditory alerting increases visual processing speed (v) and lowers perceptual threshold (t0) relative to no cue (within-subject).
results_comparison.original_results: Experiment 1: v higher with 85 dB cue vs no cue, t(27)=4.71, p<0.001, dz=0.89; t0 lower with cue, t(27)=3.52, p=0.002, dz=0.67.
results_comparison.replication_results: N=48: v (NoCue vs Cue) t=-1.3326, p=0.1891 (opposite direction: v_NoCue > v_Cue on average); t0 (NoCue vs Cue) t=1.2330, p=0.2237 (opposite direction: t0_NoCue < t0_Cue on average).
results_comparison.overall_answer: No. The replication did not satisfy preregistered criteria; effects were non-significant and in the opposite direction.

replication_report: Replication unsuccessful: No significant cue-induced increase in v or decrease in t0 (N=48); both contrasts were opposite in direction to the original Experiment 1.

failure_handling.suggestions: Apply stricter QC: exclude sessions without proper headphone use, enforce minimum trials per duration-condition, remove subjects with ceiling/near-zero accuracy, and re-estimate after trimming extreme parameter fits.

Re-implement MLE with appropriate parameter bounds (e.g., v>0, t0 bounded, pg in [0,1]) as in the original; report fit quality; exclude non-convergent or ill-fitted subjects; compare SSE vs MLE in sensitivity analyses.
Predefine outlier rules for subject parameters; provide diagnostic plots of accuracy-by-duration per condition to validate TVA assumptions; consider robust summaries (trimmed means/medians).
Harden file discovery (regex tests) and add parsing sanity checks (e.g., cue markers, duration parsing) with logs of anomalies before fitting.

notes: Subject-level table shows many negative t0 and extreme v/pg estimates, which can destabilize group comparisons. The independent dataset lacks pupillometry and may differ in hearing screening/compliance. Results may change with MLE, parameter constraints, and rigorous QC/exclusion criteria; sensitivity analyses are recommended.
